channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: A Republican-backed bill would upend voter registration. Here are 8 things to know
item.description: Congressional Republicans are pushing legislation that would make sweeping changes to voter registration, including requiring those signing up to present documents proving U.S. citizenship.
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block, MPR News

item.title: The European Union retaliates after the U.S. metals tariffs take hold
item.description: The European Union on Wednesday announced retaliatory trade action with a series of duties on U.S. products, responding to the Trump administration rise in tariffs on all steel and aluminum imports.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump campaigned as a protector of free speech. Critics say his actions as president threaten it
item.description: DENVER (AP) — When President Donald Trump gave his joint address to Congress last week, he boasted that in his first few weeks back in the White House he had “brought free speech back to America.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI and CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal judge cancels hearing in NYC Mayor Eric Adams' corruption case
item.description: MANHATTAN, N.Y. (PIX11) – A hearing in the corruption case against New York City Mayor Eric Adams has been canceled for Friday. U.S. District Judge Dale Ho, who's weighing the Justice Department's request to dismiss the charges against Adams, called the hearing off.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: First on Fox: House Republican campaign arm targets vulnerable Democrats who 'voted to shut down' government
item.description: The House GOP's campaign committee is taking aim at congressional Democrats for voting against a measure to fund the federal government through Sept. 30.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NASA spent $13M on DEI under Biden while critical mission, quality controls flailed, watchdog group says
item.description: While issues at NASA compounded under Joe Biden, the space agency continued to embed diversity, equity and inclusion initiatives into its workforce, according to a new report from watchdog group OpenTheBooks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: EPA terminates Biden admin's green grants worth $20B, Zeldin says
item.description: The EPA is terminating $20 billion in grants awarded by the Biden administration for climate and clean-energy projects, Administrator Lee Zeldin announced.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Education Department cuts half its staff as Trump vows to wind the agency down
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Education Department plans to lay off more than 1,300 of its employees as part of an effort to halve the organization’s staff -- a prelude to President Donald Trump’s plan to dismantle the agency.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY and MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judges finds a huge reduction in staff and funding would be legal at African development agency
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Tuesday found that removing most grants and most staff from a U.S. federal agency that invests in African small businesses would be legal. U.S.
item.dc:creator: THALIA BEATY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lone Democrat to back House GOP spending bill explains decision
item.description: Rep. Jared Golden (D-Maine) defied House Democratic leadership on Tuesday when he voted for the GOP’s bill to avert a government shutdown — the only one in the caucus to support the stopgap. Golden’s “yes” vote, to be sure, was not decisive, since Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Virginia’s Earle-Sears drops first ad confronting Dem opponent over migrant crime, border wall opposition
item.description: An ad from Virginia Lt. Gov. Winsome Earle-Sears plays clips of former Rep. Abigail Spanberger's comments, including her opposition to Trump's border wall, and news clippings.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DOGE protesters rally outside key department after employees are told not to report to work Wednesday
item.description: Protesters against DOGE rallied outside the Education Department and held a moment of appreciation and applause for the federal agency rumored to be the next on the chopping block.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Republican-led effort to halt climate change lawsuits in Democratic-led states
item.description: The Supreme Court has rejected a lawsuit from Republican attorneys general in 19 states aimed at blocking climate change suits against the oil and gas industry from Democratic-led states, including Minnesota.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: As Trump downplays economic fears, this data shows people feel differently
item.description: Economists look for signs that a recession may be approaching by monitoring consumer confidence and business sentiment — two indicators of uncertainty.
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman, MPR News

item.title: Trump administration halts funding for two cybersecurity efforts, including one for elections
item.description: The Trump administration has cut millions of dollars in federal funding from two cybersecurity initiatives, including one dedicated to helping state and local election officials.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: House subcommittee hears testimony on school choice
item.description: Some say parents should be able to choose how their children are educated, while others worry redirecting funds could gut public education.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Trump considers easing safety screenings for chemicals
item.description: The Trump administration will consider easing regulations under which it considers the safety of existing chemicals, drawing concerns from public health advocates.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Act to repeal harmful law may not go far enough for Dakota people
item.description: The Sioux Dakota Removal Act passed in 1863 could be repealed in South Dakota. Dakota people in Minnesota wonder if it will go far enough to undo the harm it caused when it was originally passed.
item.dc:creator: Allison Herrera, MPR News

item.title: Judge blocks deportation of Columbia University protester
item.description: A Palestinian green card holder, Khalil earned his master's degree in December, and his wife, a U.S. citizen, is eight months pregnant.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Karl Rove: Trump claims of DOGE 'fraud' discoveries will backfire
item.description: Republican strategist Karl Rove agreed with James Carville, who had urged Democrats to make a "strategic political retreat," advising them "to play dead.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, NewsNation

item.title: Protestors dispute Vance’s description of clash on street over Ukraine
item.description: In a post on X, Vance said he was confronted by pro-Ukraine protestors while he was with his 3-year-old daughter on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about Mark Carney, elected next Canadian prime minister
item.description: Mark Carney, a former central banker, won his Liberal Party's election in a landslide on Sunday to replace Justin Trudeau as prime minister of Canada. He will be sworn into office in the coming days.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Ex-central banker to replace Trudeau as Canada's prime minister after winning Liberal Party vote
item.description: Former central banker Mark Carney will become Canada’s next prime minister after the governing Liberal Party elected him its leader as the country deals with U.S. President Donald Trump’s trade war and annexation threat, and a federal election looms.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Ras Baraka has highest favorability among Democratic candidates for NJ governor: poll
item.description: NEW JERSEY (PIX11) – New polling from Fairleigh Dickinson University gave us some early insight on who voters are looking into in the race for New Jersey governor. Democratic voters weighed in on the candidates' favorability.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Social Security offices in NY set to close amid DOGE budget cuts
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Several Social Security offices in New York will be closing as the Department of Government Efficiency continues to cut federal spending. The closures come after the Social Security Administration said it found over $800 million in savings for the 2025 fiscal year.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Hillsborough commission votes against asking for state DOGE audit
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) — The newly formed Florida State Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) task force is set to use technology and AI to help look for government waste. It will audit colleges and universities, state agencies, along with local governments.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US paused Ukraine intelligence support: Trump officials
item.description: CIA Director John Ratcliffe and national security adviser Michael Waltz both said the U.S. had paused intelligence sharing in addition to military aid.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5
